summary: Iteration 5 testing complete. Verified new payment types (salary/advance/overtime/tickets/id_card), expense creation for tickets and id_card payments, and employee summary endpoint with monthly breakdown. All 14 backend tests pass. Frontend UI verification confirms Pay dialog with 5 payment type options, cash/bank mode selection, warning for tickets/id_card, and summary dialog with monthly breakdown showing balance and badges for each payment type.

issues: Console warning: HTML hydration error about <tr> inside <span> - React DevTools artifact, not functional issue (same as iteration 4)
Console warning: Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent - accessibility improvement needed

passed_tests: Backend: Login with test@example.com/password123 returns access_token
Backend: POST /api/salary-payments with payment_type=salary works
Backend: POST /api/salary-payments with payment_type=advance works
Backend: POST /api/salary-payments with payment_type=overtime works
Backend: POST /api/salary-payments with payment_type=tickets creates payment AND expense
Backend: POST /api/salary-payments with payment_type=id_card creates payment AND expense
Backend: Regular salary payment does NOT create expense
Backend: GET /api/employees/{id}/summary returns correct structure (employee, monthly_summary, total_all_time)
Backend: Summary monthly breakdown includes salary_paid, advance, overtime, tickets, id_card, balance
Backend: Summary balance calculation correct (monthly_salary - salary_paid)
Backend: Summary returns 404 for invalid employee ID
Backend: POST /api/salary-payments with payment_mode=cash works
Backend: POST /api/salary-payments with payment_mode=bank works
Backend: Summary includes payments array with id, payment_type, amount, payment_mode, date
Frontend: Employees page loads with table and stats cards
Frontend: Pay dialog has Payment Type dropdown
Frontend: Pay dialog shows all 5 payment types (Salary, Advance, Overtime, Tickets, ID Card)
Frontend: Pay dialog has Cash/Bank mode selection
Frontend: Pay dialog shows warning for tickets ('This will also be added to Expenses')
Frontend: Pay dialog shows warning for id_card
Frontend: View button opens Payment Summary dialog
Frontend: Summary shows Monthly Salary, Total Paid (All Time), Position
Frontend: Summary shows monthly breakdown with period name
Frontend: Summary shows Paid amount and Balance per period
Frontend: Summary shows badges for payment types (Salary: $X, Tickets: $X)
Frontend: Summary shows individual payment details (date, type, amount)
critical_code_review_comments: All 5 payment types correctly implemented in SalaryPayment model
Tickets and id_card payments correctly create expense records via backend
Employee summary endpoint returns complete monthly breakdown with correct balance calculation
Frontend Pay dialog properly shows all payment type options
Warning message for tickets/id_card expense creation working correctly
Summary dialog shows grouped data by period with individual payment details
seed_data_creation: Test data from previous iterations: Ahmed Khan with multiple payments (salary, tickets) across Feb 2026 period. Total Paid: $8800.00
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 complete. New features tested: 1) SalaryPayment model now has payment_type field with 5 options (salary/advance/overtime/tickets/id_card), 2) When payment_type is 'tickets' or 'id_card', backend automatically creates an Expense record with category 'tickets' or 'id_card', 3) GET /api/employees/{id}/summary returns employee info + monthly_summary array with salary_paid, advance, overtime, tickets, id_card, balance (salary - salary_paid), total_paid per period + payments array with individual transactions, 4) Frontend Pay dialog has Payment Type dropdown with all 5 options, 5) Warning text 'This will also be added to Expenses' shown for tickets/id_card, 6) Summary dialog shows monthly breakdown with colored badges per payment type. Console warnings about HTML hydration and DialogContent description are non-functional artifacts.
